steps.title: Set up the area equation
steps.content: The base is 12 cm greater than the height, so if the height is $h$, the base is $b = h + 12$. Using the triangle area formula with Area = 270: $$270 = \frac{1}{2}(h+12) \cdot h$$

steps.title: Backsolve the choices
steps.content:
The answer choices are simple integers, so we can test them directly.

Backsolving by testing the candidate (30): If $h = 30$, then $b = 30 + 12 = 42$, and Area $= \frac{1}{2}(42)(30) = 630 \neq 270$. Fails.

Testing this candidate (18): If $h = 18$, then $b = 18 + 12 = 30$, and Area $= \frac{1}{2}(30)(18) = \frac{540}{2} = 270$. This matches. That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
